title: Legal Information Services - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Legal Information Services 11 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Issues LGBTQ2+ Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ACCESS ATTORNEY PROGRAM MULTNOMAH ATTORNEY ACCESS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal information services including: -Introducing crime victims to advocates who can assist with processing trauma, navigating the rights they may have as a victim, and getting restraining orders. -Helping individuals report crimes to police or an investigator. -Providing case information and updates to impacted individuals. -Providing information on how to address prior convictions or court fines and fees. -Explaining how the criminal justice system operates. -Helping defendants connect with their defense attorney or parole/probation officer, getting information on warrant lifts, and providing upcoming court dates. -Helping people get referred to other services and other community-based organizations. Website Eligibility: Resident of neighborhood served by a MAAP attorney Hours: Not applicable (503) 988-3162 Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights and refers cases requiring legal advice or representation to attorneys at Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. Also conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. 333 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 669-8350 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORT KENNEDY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, pet food, clothing, showers, blood pressure checks, bus passes, legal information services, and benefit and housing resources. 7600 SE Johnson Creek Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Veterans, all discharge types Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm. Various programs may have specific times. Email (503) 765-2661 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LAMBDA LEGAL LEGAL HELP DESK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and resources regarding discrimination related to sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, and HIV status; inquiries are kept confidential. Assistance may include follow-up discussions with Lambda Legal attorneys or contact information for an attorney in the area or for other organizations that may directly assist. The Help Desk cannot provide legal advice. 120 Wall Street 19th Floor New York, NY 100053919 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (213) 382-7600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL CENTER FOR LGBTQ RIGHTS LEGAL INFORMATION LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance on civil and human rights issues affecting LGBTQ2+ individuals, including public policy advocacy, impact litigation, legal help, and education on legal rights. Specific online forms for custody disputes, separation or divorce, immigration or asylum, and other issues. 870 Market Street Suite 370 San Francisco, CA 94102 Eligibility: LGBTQ2+ individuals and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (800) 528-6257 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON CONSUMER JUSTICE CONSUMER RIGHTS RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tools and information to assist in exercising consumer rights. Resources include handouts, articles, videos, research, and comic books to support consumers in taking action against unlawful practices and holding companies accountable. Also provides connection to legal experts (e.g., direct representation, advice) for those facing consumer-related law issues. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 406-3311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER PORTLAND REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website offers legal information regarding various issues, including housing, family law, employment law, elder law, government benefits, and consumer protection. Provides links to legal service providers, resources, and a directory of legal aid providers. 621 SW Morrison Street Suite 1450 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE BAR LEGAL LINKS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a website containing general legal information, provided as a public service by Oregon's lawyers. The information is not intended to be legal advice regarding an individual's particular problem. None of the information is intended to replace the work of an attorney. Note that changes may occur in any of the areas of the law. 16037 SW Upper Boones Ferry Road Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and services to protect and empower the immigrant community including a hotline to report local Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) sightings, assistance with locating loved ones who have been detained or deported, education on immigrant rights including free Know Your Rights fliers and handouts, drop-in resource center, and information and referral to additional resources such as legal aid or accompaniment to check-ins at the ICE office. Also offers volunteer opportunities. 4122 S Moody Avenue Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 7:45am-noon Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 622-1510 Get Directions Visit Website More Details URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about tenant rights, eviction prevention, and legal assistance, referring cases requiring legal advice or representation by attorneys to Legal Aid Services of Oregon, Fair Housing Council of Oregon, and Oregon Law Center. Also, conducts outreach and tenant rights education in the community. Also includes culturally-specific case workers located in geographically underserved areas. 3934 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 280-2600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY LAW LIBRARY LAW LIBRARY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to print and digital collection of statutes, case law, subjects covering state and federal jurisdictions, and historic Oregon legislative materials. The library offers computers for public use and meeting spaces. 111 NE Lincoln Street Suite 250-L Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-8880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EL PROGRAMA HISPANO CATÓLICO FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE FORT KENNEDY FORT KENNEDY OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW HELP WEBSITE OREGON STATE BAR LEGAL LINKS PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION URBAN LEAGUE OF PORTLAND FAIR HOUSING ENFORCEMENT COLLABORATIVE WASHINGTON COUNTY LAW LIBRARY LAW LIBRARY
